channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump disrupts global economic order even though the US is dominant
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — By declaring a trade war on the rest of the world, President Donald Trump has panicked global financial markets, raised the risk of a recession and broken the political and economic alliances that made much of the world stable for business after World War II.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Latest: Asia shares sink as US tariffs take effect
item.description: Asian shares slipped further on Wednesday as the latest set of U.S. tariffs, including a massive 104% levy on Chinese imports, took effect. Japan’s Nikkei 225 index bounced lower, at point point losing more than 5% on Wednesday. Other markets in the region also skidded.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 96-year-old resident at southern Utah care home dies after being found in pool
item.description: A resident of a senior living facility in southern Utah has died after being found in the community's pool on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Many older AAPI adults are foreign-born. They're tough on immigration too, a new poll finds
item.description: A new poll finds sharp generational disagreements among Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders when it comes to how the U.S. should handle mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang And Amelia Thomson-Deveaux, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Republicans are going public with their growing worries about Trump's tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Manufacturers struggling to make long-term plans. Farmers facing retaliation from Chinese buyers. U.S. households burdened with higher prices.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Surgery nightmare: Hospital admits mistake after failed cancer surgery attempt
item.description: An investigation is underway at MountainView Hospital in Las Vegas after a cancer patient woke up to startling news in the operating room.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, WGN 9

item.title: Youth soccer coach charged in the death of 13-year-old found on Southern California road
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A youth soccer coach has been charged in the death of a 13-year-old whose body was found on the side of a Southern California road.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A mix of clouds and sunshine expected with slightly cooler afternoon temperatures
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) - Happy Tuesday, Utah! A weak front moved through northern Utah Tuesday morning, bringing a few more clouds, breezy conditions, and a slight chance for isolated showers north of I-80.
item.dc:creator: Alana Brophy, ABC 4

item.title: TSA Officers find hidden weapons inside cane
item.description: TSA officers at the Shreveport Regional Airport security checkpoint discovered a cache of weapons, including a gutting knife, a double-edged blade, and a knife that could function as a small machete, all hidden inside a metal cane.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Cheng, NewsNation

item.title: What's next for the 2 Utahns returning to U.S. after Congo coup conviction
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- After three Americans were convicted of participating in a failed coup attempt in the Democratic Republic of Congo, the country's presidency returned the Americans -- two of whom are from Utah -- to the United States on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: The trial of an ex-deputy who shot an unarmed Black woman in her home is moved to another city
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The murder trial of a former Illinois sheriff's deputy who fatally shot Sonya Massey, an unarmed black woman, in her Springfield home last summer will be moved out of the capital city to Peoria because of intense publicity, a judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Agreement in works to settle Northwestern hazing lawsuits that led to football coach's firing
item.description: Former Northwestern University football players are finalizing an agreement with the school to settle lawsuits alleging hazing and abuse on the team that led to longtime coach Pat Fitzgerald’s firing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Waiver allows Trump administration to bypass environmental rules to build more border walls
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — A waiver issued Tuesday by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security allows the federal government to bypass environmental regulations and begin construction to add more barriers along the U.S-Mexico border in Southern California even as illegal crossings have plummeted.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump touts Supreme Court deportation ruling as a major victory, but legal fight is far from over
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is touting a Supreme Court ruling allowing it to resume deportations under the Alien Enemies Act as a major victory, but the immigration fight is far from over.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing language translations of its products, a change that experts say could put non-English speakers at risk of missing potentially life-saving warnings about extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Future uncertain for Duke basketball star due to new Trump policy
item.description: A new visa policy announced by the Trump administration last weekend could impact South Sudanese passport holders, including Duke basketball phenom Khaman Maluach.
item.dc:creator: Keaton Eberly, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jeffries accuses Trump of tanking the economy to create opportunities for the rich
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Monday accused President Trump of intentionally crashing the economy to benefit the GOP’s wealthy donors and other well-heeled interests.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Spanberger raises $6.7 million in Virginia governor race
item.description: Former Rep. Abigail Spanberger’s (D-Va.) gubernatorial campaign said Monday it raised $6.7 million in the first quarter of the year. The haul included more than 50,000 donations of $100 or less in the first three months of the year, according to the Spanberger campaign.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court rules in Trump's favor on deportations and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: RFK Jr. plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: The addition of low levels of fluoride to drinking water was long considered one of the greatest public health achievements of the last century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How Philadelphia police draw on personal experiences to respond to mental health crises
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — On a frigid February morning, Vanity Cordero, a Philadelphia police officer, heard a call over the radio for a man threatening to jump from a bridge. The details sounded familiar.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kentucky watches for surging rivers to recede so widespread cleanup can begin
item.description: After days of deluges overfilled rivers to near-record levels across Kentucky, residents are anxious to return to their flooded homes and assess what is salvageable.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner And Kristin M. Hall, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Scholarships for child care are drying up. Now families are paying the price
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — For parents who need to work but can't afford the steep cost of child care, federally funded scholarships can be a lifeline. Delivered through state child care assistance programs, the scholarships can mean the difference between a parent working full time — or not at all.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal magistrate weighs arguments in Idaho news groups' execution access lawsuit
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — A federal magistrate judge is expected to hear arguments Tuesday morning in a lawsuit brought by three news organizations that say Idaho prison officials are unconstitutionally hiding parts of lethal injection executions from public view.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's DHS revokes legal status for migrants who entered the US on Biden-era CBP One app
item.description: MCALLEN, Texas (AP) — Migrants who were temporarily allowed to live in the United States by using a Biden-era online appointment app have been told to leave the country “immediately,” officials said Monday. It was unclear how many beneficiaries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A Florida man is executed for killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break
item.description: A man convicted of killing a Miami Herald employee who was carjacked on her lunch break has been executed in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Curt Anderson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Clem Burke, multifaceted drummer of iconic rock group Blondie, has died
item.description: Clem Burke, whose versatile drumming propelled the iconic rock group Blondie during its decades performing everything from new-wave punk to disco-infused tunes, has died. He was 70. The band said in a statement on its website Monday that he died from cancer but no additional details were provided.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. says he plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — U.S. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Monday said he plans to tell the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to stop recommending fluoridation in communities nationwide.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Utah Red Cross volunteers deployed to assist with flooding, tornado relief in several other states
item.description: Volunteers with the American Red Cross of Utah disaster team have been deployed to several states that have been affected by recent tornadoes and flooding in the Midwest and South.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Swollen rivers are flooding towns in the US South after a prolonged deluge of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Days of unrelenting downpours swelled rivers to near record levels across Kentucky on Monday, submerging neighborhoods and threatening a famed bourbon distillery in the state capital.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump honors Dodgers' 2024 World Series championship team
item.description: (KTLA) -- President Donald Trump welcomed Shohei Ohtani, Mookie Betts and the reigning champion Los Angeles Dodgers to the White House on Monday to congratulate them for winning the World Series last season. "That is a very good-looking group of people," Trump said as he began his remarks.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses order to return Maryland dad mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: Chief Justice Roberts agreed to pause a deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FCC asks for public input on local broadcast regulations
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is asking the public to help rethink its regulation of local broadcasters. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr announced the "Delete, delete, delete" initiative last month, urging citizens to suggest which regulations should be revised or eliminated.
item.dc:creator: KTLA 5 News, KTLA 5

item.title: Reversible damage found in marathon runners' brains: study
item.description: A 26.2-mile run can do some damage to the brain, a study shows, but the impact is temporary and reversible.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, Fox 5

item.title: Judge dismisses former Mississippi governor's defamation lawsuit against news outlet
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — A judge has dismissed a lawsuit by former Mississippi Gov. Phil Bryant that claimed a local news outlet defamed him in public comments about its Pulitzer Prize-winning reporting on the misspending of $77 million in federal welfare funds.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
